message:
test(search): regression coverage for federated multi-shard lexical
bundle + minor formatting and error-context polish

Follow-up to the federated multi-shard reader / publish-manifest work
(commits bd493536, 1b48d17e, fa8525c6, 69377e83). Adds two end-to-end
tests that pin down the contract a `SearchClient` sees when a bundle
contains multiple shard indexes instead of a single merged root index,
and cleans up two spots that were doing manual error propagation or
long-line wrapping by hand.

`src/search/query.rs`
* New `search_client_reads_federated_lexical_bundle_as_one_corpus`
  builds two shard indexes (`shard-a`, `shard-b`), publishes them as a
  federated bundle, and asserts that a `SearchClient` opened against
  the published path treats the union as one searchable corpus —
  documents from both shards appear in a single query result, hit
  counts match the sum of the shards, and nothing leaks the
  shard-local `DocAddress` space to callers.
* Tidies the rustfmt drift in the federated reader-open call site
  (single-expression chain instead of the previous nested-indent
  shape) introduced when the federated reader path first landed.
* Inlines the `Drop for SearchClient` body so the reader-cache
  eviction is a single straight-line expression; removes a trivially
  reformatted block there and reduces noise when grepping for
  federated cache sites.

`src/search/tantivy.rs`
* New `publish_federated_searchable_index_directories_writes_manifest_\
  without_root_meta` verifies the on-disk layout of a federated
  publish: both shards remain fully self-contained Tantivy indexes,
  the published root has a `federated-search-manifest.json` but does
  *not* gain a root-level `meta.json` (which would make older readers
  think the root itself is a searchable index and double-count docs),
  and the manifest enumerates both shard directories.
* Replaces the hand-rolled `map_fs_err` conversion inside
  `searchable_index_summary` for the `searchable_segment_metas()` call
  with an `anyhow::Context` attachment ("reading searchable segment
  metadata for Tantivy summary") so the error bubble clearly points
  at which summary field failed instead of pretending the whole
  summary call failed up top.
* Reformats `load_federated_search_manifest_internal`'s signature to
  rustfmt's wrapped form so future edits don't churn the line.

No behaviour changes in production code paths — the two new tests fail
if the federated reader contract regresses, and the two minor cleanups
are pure formatting / error-context polish.
